title: Loss on Purchase Commitment
event: Hollis Co. has a noncancelable contract to buy raw materials next year for $50,000. At year-end, the market price of those materials has fallen to $44,000. Prepare the required entry.

lines.label: 50,000 − 44,000
lines.why: The contract obligates a purchase above market — the loss is recognized when the price falls, not at delivery.
lines.trap: No inventory exists yet, but the LOSS does — noncancelable + price decline = accrue now.

lines.label: obligation recognized
lines.why: A liability for the excess of contract price over market.
lines.trap: At delivery next year, inventory records at the 44,000 market and this liability clears against the 50,000 paid.

memorize.body: Same conservatism as LCNRV, applied one step earlier in the pipeline — to goods you've promised to buy but don't yet hold.
If the price RECOVERS before delivery, the loss can be reversed — one of the rare write-ups GAAP permits, capped at the original loss.

questions.prompt: Contract price $50,000, year-end market $44,000, noncancelable. Loss recognized THIS year = ?

distractors.feedback: Waiting for delivery ignores a committed, measurable loss. Noncancelable contract + price decline → accrue 6,000 now.
That's the market value of the goods. The LOSS is only the excess committed above it: 6,000.

build.scaffold: Contract price minus market price = the loss, accrued before any goods arrive.
